title: Designing Games
creator: Tynan Sylvester
description: A designer-led account of how rules, uncertainty, rewards, fiction, and social context combine into experiences. It moves between general principles and detailed examples rather than prescribing one universal design formula.
useful: Strong for readers who understand basic mechanics but want to reason more precisely about emotions, choices, and player stories.
audience: Intermediate designers trying to connect system design with emotion and player interpretation.
learning: Use fiction and rules as mutually supporting tools
